records.ety: [F. rversion, L. reversio a turning back. See Revert.]

defs: The act of returning, or coming back; return.
quotes: After his reversion home, [he] was spoiled, also, of all that he brought with him.

defs: That which reverts or returns; residue.
quotes: The small reversion of this great navy which came home might be looked upon by religious eyes as relics.

defs: The returning of an estate to the grantor or his heirs, by operation of law, after the grant has terminated; hence, the residue of an estate left in the proprietor or owner thereof, to take effect in possession, by operation of law, after the termination of a limited or less estate carved out of it and conveyed by him.
Hence, a right to future possession or enjoyment; succession.
quotes: For even reversions are all begged before.

defs: A payment which is not to be received, or a benefit which does not begin, until the happening of some event, as the death of a living person.
A return towards some ancestral type or character; atavism.
